perturbation.original_text: You authorize the Company to instruct Prime Trust or any escrow agent used by Company to deduct such fees, debts and any other amounts liabilities incurred under this Service Agreement, prior to releasing any amounts due to you or to any other person (including another escrow agent) from escrow.
perturbation.changed_text: You authorize the Company, at its sole discretion, to instruct Prime Trust or any escrow agent used by Company to deduct such fees, debts and any other amounts liabilities incurred under this Service Agreement. However, the timing and amount of such deductions are subject to further negotiation and mutual agreement between the Company and the Customer prior to releasing any amounts due to you or to any other person (including another escrow agent) from escrow.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates deduction of fees from escrow. The modified version introduces 'at its sole discretion' and requires 'further negotiation and mutual agreement,' making the deduction of fees uncertain and dependent on negotiation, which contradicts the original mandatory deduction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Monthly Fees and Billing The Company will bill you monthly for the Services. You authorize the Company, at its sole discretion, to instruct Prime Trust or any escrow agent used by Company to deduct such fees, debts and any other amounts liabilities incurred under this Service Agreement. However, the timing and amount of such deductions are subject to further negotiation and mutual agreement between the Company and the Customer prior to releasing any amounts due to you or to any other person (including another escrow agent) from escrow. Amounts which remain unpaid for 30 days are subject to a finance charge of 1.5% per month on any outstanding balance, or the maximum permitted by law, whichever is lower, plus all expenses of collection and may result in immediate termination of Service. You shall be responsible for all taxes associated with Services other than U.S. taxes based on the Company's net income.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a requirement for 'further negotiation and mutual agreement' before deducting fees, while the contradicted text states that amounts remaining unpaid for 30 days are subject to finance charges and potential termination. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text makes the deduction of fees uncertain and subject to negotiation, while the contradicted text implies a mandatory and timely deduction, with penalties for non-payment. This would cause confusion and potential disputes over the timing and amount of fee deductions, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to earlier termination as provided below, this Service Agreement is for the total duration of the Company's Offering (the "Initial Term") unless either party requests termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to earlier termination as provided below, this Service Agreement is for the total duration of the Company's Offering (the "Initial Term"). Either party may terminate at any time without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires 30 days' notice for termination unless there is an earlier termination based on the conditions stated in the sections. The modified version states that either party may terminate at any time without prior notice, directly contradicting the notice period and the conditions stated in the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Survival a) Subject to earlier termination as provided below, this Service Agreement is for the total duration of the Company's Offering (the "Initial Term"). Either party may terminate at any time without prior notice. b) Additionally, either party may terminate this Service Agreement in the event: i) The other party's material breach that remains not cured and continues for a period of (A) in the case of a failure involving the payment of any undisputed amount due hereunder, 15 days and (B) in the case of any other failure, 30 days after the non performing party receives notice from the terminating party specifying such failure; ii) Any statement, representation or warranty of the other party is untrue or misleading in any material respect or omits material information; iii) The other party (A) voluntarily or involuntarily is subject to bankruptcy proceedings, (B) applies for or consents to the appointment of a receiver, trustee, custodian, sequestrator, or similar official, (C) makes a general assignment to creditors, (D) commences winding down or liquidation of its business affairs, (E) otherwise takes corporate action for the purpose of effecting any of the foregoing, or (F) ceases operating in the normal course of business; iv) If any change to, enactment of, or change in interpretation or enforcement of any law occurs that would have a material adverse effect upon a party's ability to perform its obligations under this Service Agreement or a party's costs/revenues with respect to the services under this Service Agreement; v) Upon direction to a party from any regulatory authority or National Automated Clearing House Association to cease or materially limit the exercise or performance of such party's rights or obligations under this Service Agreement; vi) If there shall have occurred a material adverse change in the financial condition of the other party; or vii) Upon a force majeure event that materially prevents or impedes a party from performing its obligations hereunder for a period of more than 10 business days. StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc. Customer: By: /s/ Dawn Dickson, By:Dawn Dickson, CEO and Founder Date: 08/19/2019 /s/ Howard Marks Howard Marks CEO
perturbation.justification: The original text requires a 30-day notice for termination, unless conditions for earlier termination exist. The changed text allows termination at any time without notice. The contradicted text initially mirrors the original text by stating termination is subject to earlier termination. However, it also outlines specific conditions for termination (material breach, untrue representations, bankruptcy, etc.) requiring notice and cure periods in some cases. The changed text, by allowing termination at any time without notice, directly contradicts both the initial 30-day notice requirement (removed in the change) and the specific conditions for termination outlined in the contradicted text, where notice and cure periods are required. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as it's unclear which termination provisions apply.

perturbation.original_text: Customer authorizes the Company to take any of the following actions (in any particular order): (i) collect the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability from funds sent to your third party escrow account; (ii) debit your bank account in the amount of the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; (iv) engage in collection efforts to recover the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability and/or (v) take legal action or any other action under this Service Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer authorizes the Company to take any of the following actions (in any particular order): (i) collect the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability from funds sent to your third party escrow account; (ii) debit your bank account in the amount of the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; (iv) engage in collection efforts to recover the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; however, taking legal action is prohibited under this service agreement
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the customer authorizes the company to take legal action. The modified version contradicts the original version as the customers authorizes the company to collect unpaid portions, debit bank accounts but it is being stated that taking legal action is prohibited under this service agreement, contradicting the previous authorization for legal action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Customer authorizes the Company to take any of the following actions (in any particular order): (i) collect the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability from funds sent to your third party escrow account; (ii) debit your bank account in the amount of the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; (iv) engage in collection efforts to recover the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; however, taking legal action is prohibited under this service agreement. 5. Term and Survival a) Subject to earlier termination as provided below, this Service Agreement is for the total duration of the Company's Offering (the "Initial Term") unless either party requests termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. b) Additionally, either party may terminate this Service Agreement in the event: i) The other party's material breach that remains not cured and continues for a period of (A) in the case of a failure involving the payment of any undisputed amount due hereunder, 15 days and (B) in the case of any other failure, 30 days after the non performing party receives notice from the terminating party specifying such failure; ii) Any statement, representation or warranty of the other party is untrue or misleading in any material respect or omits material information; iii) The other party (A) voluntarily or involuntarily is subject to bankruptcy proceedings, (B) applies for or consents to the appointment of a receiver, trustee, custodian, sequestrator, or similar official, (C) makes a general assignment to creditors, (D) commences winding down or liquidation of its business affairs, (E) otherwise takes corporate action for the purpose of effecting any of the foregoing, or (F) ceases operating in the normal course of business; iv) If any change to, enactment of, or change in interpretation or enforcement of any law occurs that would have a material adverse effect upon a party's ability to perform its obligations under this Service Agreement or a party's costs/revenues with respect to the services under this Service Agreement; v) Upon direction to a party from any regulatory authority or National Automated Clearing House Association to cease or materially limit the exercise or performance of such party's rights or obligations under this Service Agreement; vi) If there shall have occurred a material adverse change in the financial condition of the other party; or vii) Upon a force majeure event that materially prevents or impedes a party from performing its obligations hereunder for a period of more than 10 business days. StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc. Customer: By: /s/ Dawn Dickson, By:Dawn Dickson, CEO and Founder Date: 08/19/2019 /s/ Howard Marks Howard Marks CEO
perturbation.justification: The original text authorizes the company to take legal action. The changed text explicitly prohibits legal action. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself as it contradicts itself, thus leading to in-text contradiction.
